crushed, as if some animals had fed upon them.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The waters of a clear spring that bursts from under the ground here keep this
area lively and vibrant. Many bushes grow around the spring and even a few trees
have found a home here, growing almost as high as they might in a forest. Tracks
of animals cover the ground all around the spring, as this is apparently their
favourite watering spot.
name: Golden Plains

desc:
The terrain undulates softly. Barely noticeable hills and valleys between them
make the landscape somewhat less boring than it might appear from a distance and
also somewhat harder to traverse. There is not enough grass to cover the ground
completely, but there are many small bushes growing all around.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Dead trees cover the landscape, indicative of a water shortage here. The wind
gusts, blowing dust and soil around. There is little vegetation here, other than
stubborn tufts of yellow-brown grass. However, there are signs of sparse animal
habitation.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
A hill with a flat top rises amid the vast expanse of plains that continues in
every direction. A small spring bubbles on the top of the hill, as a steady
stream of water flows down the southern hillside. Animal tracks are everywhere
as the animals come here to drink.
name: On a Flat Hilltop

desc:
Golden and green, the grass sways as the wind constantly pounds it, bending its
stems back and forth. Small bushes lose their leaves as the powerful wind beats
upon them. A great old tree stands here, creaking dangerously as the wind hurls
itself upon its branches.
name: Windy Plains

desc:
The plains continue in all directions. There are no trees around, but there are
many stringy-looking bushes with brownish leaves and knotted roots which stick
out of the ground. The grass that grows here has thin green stems with closely
fitting leaves and sometimes a tiny white flower on the top.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grass here is so thick that in places its strands tangle together forming
knots and thickets. Some of these knots appear flattened and pressed to the
ground, as if somebody had stepped upon them or an animal had slept there at
night. A couple of pine trees flank a small patch of bare ground where the grass
appears to have been burnt out, apparently the site of some traveller's
campfire.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The land here is level and treeless, but below the dense carpet of grass there
appear to be many tree-roots in the ground. Some properties of the climate or of
the soil of these plains must have allowed these roots to stay hidden, intact
and undecayed long after the trees themselves were gone until winds, rains or
roots of plants eroded the top layer of the ground, exposing the ancient
tree-roots.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Winds blow through the plains incessantly, their strong gusts bending and
tearing the gentle grass and making every tree sway and creak as the trunk
refuses to move together with the branches. Even the shortest bushes are almost
pulled out of the ground by the powerful winds.
name: Windy Plains

desc:
Winds that blow constantly through these plains have carried away most of the
top layer of the soil, exposing sand and clay that lay below. Clumps of grass
and rare clusters of bushes cling to the barren ground. While occasional
tree-roots in the ground indicate that this area was once home to many trees,
the few trees that do grow here now appear skinny and fragile, their thin
branches barely supporting brownish leaves.
name: Sparse Plains

desc:
Little vegetation grows in this dry, sparsely populated area. Few animals dare
roam here, as there is nary a drop of water to be found, and food is very
scarce. Years of erosion have taken their toll on the soil, reducing it to
blowing sand and small, dry patches of sickly-looking dirt.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The view from here is breathtaking. You can gaze upon the mighty Misty Mountains
in the west and upon the magnificent forest of Lorien that rises to the north
and to the east. To the south you can see a wide plain stretch out to the very
horizon. You can look in all directions as nothing hinders your vision.
name: Atop the Trees

desc:
Tall trees rise all around the barn. A few ladders and ropes can be seen in the
tree-crowns, leading from branch to branch mostly, as it seems, into nowhere, as
the dense foliage masks most destinations making it impossible to see anything
except leaves and branches. Nestled in the tree-branches slightly above the
level of the barn one can see two platforms to the east and to the west that
appear to be reachable from the roof. A thin board forms a slim bridge that
leads to the eastern platform while the platform to the west can be reached by
climbing the tree-branches. A small shack can be seen on the roof to the north.
name: On Top of the Barn

desc:
A small wooden chest sits in the corner of this platform, its back against the
trunk of the tree. Looking over the edge of the platform, you can tell that you
are high above the ground. Slightly below the eastern edge of the platform you
can see the top of a wooden building but everything below the level of its roof
is concealed by branches and leaves. The slightest breeze that passes through
the trees seems magnified here as it makes all leaves rustle loudly.
name: A Wooden Platform

desc:
A small wooden platform without any railing sits in the branches of an enormous
tree, one branch successfully supporting the entire weight of the platform.
Except for a sea of branches and leaves there is nothing you can see above,
around or below, except to the west where a wooden building rises among the
trees. A slender bridge made just of one board leads from the platform to the
roof of the building slightly below.
name: Platform in a Tree

desc:
The walls and the roof of this small shack are of a dark wood. There are no
windows as this is apparently not a dwelling but a storage place. A low desk
sits in a corner. In another corner there is a hole in a floor. Attached to an
iron ring in the wall, a rope descends into the hole, apparently intended as a
quick exit down to the forest floor.
name: A Small Shack

desc:
This small cavern has been dug so carelessly that it is a marvel that it has not
collapsed at all. It has apparently been used by orcs as a storage place, and
the slovenly nature of orcs can be seen both in its construction and in its
contents. Bones are strewn all over the uneven floor together with many bits and
pieces of broken armour and remainders of food and drink.
name: Storage Pit

desc:
This cavern is little more than a hole in the ground and one can tell that it
had probably been dug not so long ago. The earthen walls are uneven as they
appear to have been quickly dug out without any care for the appearance. In the
northern wall of the cavern there is a dark opening.
name: Underground Cavern

desc:
The rock-pebbled hills have given way to mountains here. There are cliffs and
jagged precipices all around. Sharp, rocky flints lie underfoot. No plant life
or foliage can be seen growing, with the exception of some crabgrass which has
pushed its roots between the cracks of the stones and cliffs. The mountains form
a narrow pass to the east, while a massive stone towers to the south.
name: Mountainside

desc:
A narrow pass runs between the cliffs. High walls of stone rise up to the north
and the south, their jagged edges covered with moss. Loose, rolling rocks and
pebbles crumble underfoot, forcing travellers to choose their way carefully. A
few spiny weeds have pushed their roots through the cracks in the stone. Their
slender branches wave in the wind. A thin, long crack runs vertically in a rock
wall to the south, forming a small slit.
name: Narrow Pass

desc:
The hills have coalesced into a mountain here. The dark stone is full of jagged
edges and steep cliffs. To the west, the cliffs narrow so that there is only a
small way visible between the mountain walls. To the north, the mountain
abruptly ends at a steep cliff, discouraging from any travelling to this
direction.
name: Mountainside

desc:
Hills form the base of a mountain here. Bare patches of stone cover some of the
hills, while others have a cover of grass or dirt. To the west, the mountain
quickly ascends high above the hills and fields around, seeming like a sudden
change to this otherwise calm landscape.
name: Foothills

desc:
The land starts sloping upwards to the west, still covered with lush grass. Some
trees grow on the top of the hill, and their branches are bent by passing winds.
Flowers thrive up from the undergrowth, blooming in various colours. To the
west, the foothills of a mountain continue, and further to the south, a small
village sits in the middle of the grass-filled lands.
name: Grassy Hills

desc:
Large shrubs dominate the view over these flat plains. Accompanied by a fair
layer of grass, they form thick undergrowth. The prickly stems of the shrubs and
plants could easily tangle the foot of a traveller, slowing their progress. A
few lone trees stand like pillars between the shrubs. To the west, the terrain
begins to slope upwards, as the grassy fields lead further to the foothills.
name: Dense Plains

desc:
Long and vibrant grass covers these fields with a seemingly never-ending green
wave. Small trees have sprung up. Their branches reach towards the sky, but not
many of them manage to grow between the large amounts of grass. The northern
lands are more densely populated with trees and a faint trail leads off into the
forest.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
The land stretches out as flat fields of grass. Small bushes and trees grow
eventually coalescing towards the north, at the edge of a small woodland area.
The grass continues as far as the eye can see, waving in the passing winds like
a green sea. Blooming flowers sprout up from the grass, tempting little insects
to feed on them.
name: Fields near a Trail

desc:
The trail widens as its surface becomes well-trodden and packed with dirt. No
plants or tufts of weeds grow on the path, giving it this often-travelled look.
A flat landscape surrounds the area, forming seemingly endless seas of grassy
plains. Green bushes grow here and there sideways with the trail, carrying
various berries. To the west, the pathway enters the grassy fields that surround
these areas for miles.
name: Dusty Trail

desc:
A thin layer of grass grows on the dirt-covered trail. Small plants spring up
from the rutted tracks. The grass grows thick and high beside the path. Some
trees have taken root alongside the path and hang above, often providing shade
and a good resting place for weary travellers. The trail makes a turn here as it
enters dull-coloured plains to the south.
name: Dusty Trail in the Grass

desc:
Small bushes cluster in miniature groves on these plains full of healthy and
green grass. The trees are large and strong, indicating their age. Miniscule
plants sprout up from the knee-high and thick cover of grass, often tangling the
foot of a passing mount or traveller. To the west, the edge of a trail can be
spotted, and to the north, the lush sea of grass continues.
name: Grassy Coppice

desc:
The trail is dusty and uneven with plant roots poking out of the thick, dirty
surface. Some small bushes slightly block the trail, while grassland closes in
from the north and the east. Shrubs sprout up at the edges of the trail, and a
few flowers dot the starting grassland. To the south, the pathway continues,
meandering through the grassland.
name: Dusty Forest Trail

desc:
The trail widens as the trees and bushes grow less densely, giving room to the
grass that pushes in from the south. The packed, dirty surface of the path is
covered with dead leaves and old pine needles that have fallen from the trees. A
few shrubs and tufts of trampled grass peek out of the dirt, cushioning the
travellers' footsteps.
name: Pine Forest Trail

desc:
The trail runs through a stand of pine and oak trees which form a green canopy
overhead. Some large bushes line the edges of the path, at a few places
encroaching on the trail and forcing travellers to take greater care passing by.
To the north, the trail becomes more difficult to walk on. Untamed grassland and
fields are located to the south and the west.
name: Pine Trail

desc:
A green carpet of grass sways on these plains like a gigantic sea. Numerous
patches of blooming plants and shrubs dot the greenery in a cacophony of
colours. A few thin and tall trees grow on the grassy cover, providing a strong
contrast to the short shrubbery. To the east, a trail surrounded by pines runs
on its course.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The long verdant grass is abundantly interspersed with small plants. Flowers dot
the carpet of green, and a few trees grow here and there, their branches hanging
low towards the ground. Some bushy patches cover portions of the field, giving
it a pockmarked look. To the south, the land slightly begins to rise.
name: Lush Plains

desc:
The plains mainly consist of dirt here, with a few tufts of grass sprinkled
around. Rocks are the primary feature. Some stones are seemingly placed by an
invisible hand. To the south, some rocky hills rise up from the flat plains.
They form a strong contrast to the area around here that slopes up and down in
small knolls only.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The plains are covered with rocks and boulders, forming a collage of stones and
dirt. To the south, the mountain rises almost straight up, blocking any attempt
at passage. To the north, the plains are lush with green grass and large bushes.
To the east and the west, the ground is flat. Pebbled fields of grass are
everything that can be seen in these directions.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The land is flat but has a fair amount of foliage and greenery. The plants seem
to be healthy and thriving. Many bushes grow in the area. To the south, a wall
of mountains rises up almost vertically, preventing any further travelling in
this direction. Westwards, the ground slopes upwards, forming small rocky hills
which lead up to the mountains.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
The ground slopes upwards, forming a rocky hillside. A thin layer of dirt covers
the stony surface, only offering the least demanding plants and shrubs enough
nutrients to survive. Patches of moss grow on the sides of chunky stones and
boulders, which are both piled up or scattered over the area. To the south, the
mountainside becomes higher, making the journey more exhausting in this
direction.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The grass gives way to dirt and rocky pebbles here. Large boulders lie on the
plains. Some are randomly scattered, some seemingly placed according to a
strange design. Between the scanted grass tufts, the grey colour of rocks and
moss dominates, giving the area a barren look. To the south, the predominance of
grey colours continues, as rocky hills rise up towards a mountainside.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Lush grass grows in abundance. Blades wave in the passing breezes, almost
constantly producing rustling noises. Bushes of green and brown colour and
little flowers with their colourful leaves and blue and red blooms dot the
undergrowth. The soil seems to be soft and wet, making the ground soggy. Upon
closer examination, a small spring of clear water can be seen spurting out of
the ground, watering the surrounding fields.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
The plains stay flat for miles, dominated by various shades of green grass. The
waves of the greenery rise up two feet with smaller plants growing amidst the
tall blades. Tiny, white flowers dot the waving carpet, while bees and other
insects fly around them collecting their nectar. A few animals can often be seen
scurrying through the grass. To the south, the land becomes rocky, while the sea
of grass flows on in other directions.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
The land slopes up and down in small hillocks. The grass covers the fields with
a dark shade of green. Here and there, patches of dirt interrupt the grass with
brown spots. Some weeds spring up in various places across the field, and a few
crabgrass plants can be spotted peeking up from the dirt.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
Green grass provides a bright contrast to the duller shades of green of the
forest to the east. Some small shrubberies dots the plain at various places.
Trees are scarce but some can be seen to the north and the east. To the west,
the land opens up in a broad expanse of plains.
name: Lush Plains

desc:
The dirty trail runs along the small edge of a forest here. The landscape to the
west and the south is flat and covered with lush grass. Some trees grow here and
there around the trail, becoming denser to the north-west. A few shrubs peek out
of the surface of the path, covering it with trampled stems of grass.
name: Trail Close to a Forest

desc:
The trail narrows as it gets closer to the forest. Knotted roots of trees
meander in and out of the path, seemingly wanting to ensnare a careless
traveller. Thick and spiny shrubs grow along the edges of the trail. A few
fallen and rotten trees also help to prevent any fast movement through this
landscape. To the north, the trees grow closer to each other, leading deeper
into the forest.
name: An Overgrown Part of the Trail

desc:
Tall pine trees fill the forested area, filling the air with a sweet odour. A
brown bed of soft pine needles covers the ground. Green plants sprout up between
the cover of needles. A few bushes have planted their roots into this calm
environment. A small trail runs further to the west and the south, leading to
the edge of a forest.
name: Pine Forest

desc:
Long green grass covers the landscape. Its vibrant and healthy stems rise
skyward, reaching almost two feet in height. Small plants and shrubs grow
between tufts of grass, almost struggling for gaining space in this thick carpet
of growth. Looking westwards, pine trees belonging to woodlands dot the horizon.
name: In the Long Grass

desc:
Large bushes are spread out amidst the abundant and thick grass. Small trees
rise up here and there, growing together with lush bushes that shoot up between
the blades of grass. Prickly nettles and briars mix with the grass and little
blue-white flowers that reach up for sunlight. Small insects buzz around, and
tiny animals can often be seen scurrying around while searching for food. To the
west, the grass appears to be growing higher.
name: Abundant Grassland

desc:
The grass is fertile and thrives, presenting itself in healthy shades of green.
To the north, foothills can be seen leading up to the distant and high
mountains. Small plants and shrubs grow between the grass, and the bushes paint
an epic picture of natural beauty. To the south, the land becomes extremely
barren. The ground flattens to the east, as the plains extend in this direction.
name: Plains near the Foothills

desc:
The flat lands are overgrown by plants and other foliage. However, the dark
green shade of grass seems to be the most dominating life. Scattered groups of
little white flowers appear at some places, providing some contrast to the
sombre green. Small chunks of rock and pebbles interrupt the sea of grass as
well. Each of them is covered with moss.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
The ground is soft, and the rich soil welcomes the amber and green grass that
grows here in abundance. Small plants and flowers have firmly placed their roots
in the ground, giving the impression of small ornaments in the grass. The tracks
of a few wild animals can be spotted here and there, criss-crossing the field.
To the south, the land becomes bushy, with a few trees standing close to the
horizon.
name: Verdant Plains

desc:
The calm landscape, covered with low grass, looks like a swaying sea of dark
green colour. Bushes and small clumps of shrubs grow scattered over the area. A
few flowers peek out of the grass with blooms of various shades. To the east, a
visible trail passes by, surrounded by a few trees and bushes.
name: Bushy Plains near a Trail

desc:
An earthen pathway meanders along the edge of this land, dotted with several
trees. The undergrowth and the trees themselves grow denser to the east, where a
small grove has formed over time. The trail leads to the east as it runs closer
to the coppice, meandering between trees, bushes and foliage. Around the edges
of the path, grows green vibrant grass that spreads on the plains that lies to
the west and the south.
name: A Tree-dotted Trail

desc:
This earthen trail runs along the forest trees. It winds back and forth amidst
the undergrowth and shrubbery. Several twisty roots of trees stick out of the
dirty cover of the pathway. The woodland continues towards the east, while the
path leads southwards with a small turn.
name: A Trail Passing the Forest

desc:
Tall and aged oaks grow here, mixed with broad-leafed trees and a few young
pines. The dense undergrowth and spiny shrubbery make travelling here difficult.
A layer of twisted roots and dead leaves covers the ground. The trees grow
sparser to the east, and a small trail can be spotted leading to the south.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
This woodland area is sparsely populated with young trees, a mix of pine and
cedar. Some small bushes and shrubs dot the undergrowth. A few forest flowers
have planted their roots in the soil, just provided with enough light and
nutrients for their needs. To the east, the forest seems to fade, giving way to
the bush-filled grassland.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Small bushes abound here, providing a break to the monotony of the short green
turf. Amidst the waves of grass, there grow small foliage plants, shrubs, and
red and white flowers. A burnt and ashen tree stands alone in the middle of the
plains, hollowed out over time. To the west, tall trees rise up towards the sky.
name: Grasslands near a Forest

desc:
Small hills rise and fall upon the surrounding land, making the landscape look
wavy. Trees top the higher points of the hillocks, growing up from between the
healthy grass and plants. Bushes and shrubs flood the small ravines, and grass
grows knee-high. To the west, a small group of trees catches the eye.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The bark of the tree is gnarled and coarse, offering fair support to anyone who
has decided to climb up here. The lush green leaves rustle in passing breezes,
their colour varying from dark to light, depending on the time of the day, and
therefore the warm rays of sun or the cold and shallow moonbeams spreading over
the surrounding lands.
name: On the Branches of the Tree

desc:
Standing on slightly higher ground, this patch of brush and light woodland looks
down over Nimrodel, where she meets with a southerly tributary before flowing
onward to the northeast. The land here is fairly fertile, no longer a part of
the mountains proper, but approaching the lowlands wherein lies the vast expanse
of Lothlorien, the Golden Wood. And indeed, this very copse marks the furthest
western boundary of that forest, although it is too far from the heart of Lorien
for any mellyrn to be found here.
name: At the Edge of the Forest

desc:
As she travels onward to the east, Nimrodel becomes ever more gentle in her
progress. The lands through which she journeys are steadily becoming flatter and
more fertile, and already she finds herself entering an area of brush and light
woodland. The cold, clear waters, said by some to be healing to the weary,
remain shallow here, and the stream might easily be forded by any who chose to
do so.
name: Along a Shallow Stream

desc:
The land falls away gently to the east and south, where scrubby brushwood grows,
marking the beginnings of a vast forest. And through this part of the woods
flows a sparkling stream. Succoured by her waters, the ground here, whilst still
quite rocky and sloped, is able to support a fairly thick area of bushes and
shrubs, as well as bright wildflowers and dark green grass.
name: Beside a Lively Stream

desc:
A prominent ridgeline comes to a faltering end here, where it divides a fertile
little valley to the north from some craggier terrain to the south. Rising
sharply, the land climbs westward toward a mountain peak. To the east it falls
away quite rapidly, as it approaches the outskirts of the Golden Wood, and the
far distant waters of the Great River.
name: Climbing Toward a Ridge

desc:
The valley floor slopes gently away from you to the north and east. To the west
a steady climb up the narrowing coomb lies ahead of you, and southward is a
steeper trek, as the land rises toward the encircling arms of a mountain ridge.
A small stand of trees grows on the lower reaches of the valley floor, but here
only a few wildflowers and small shrubs can make a living.
name: In a Sloping Valley

desc:
Nimrodel runs slowly, her waters swirling into shallow, wide iridescent pools of
aquamarine. The stream is flanked by a copse of proud and verdant oaks, their
mighty boughs drooping over the streambed laden with the weight of the lushly
growing foliage. To the north, the oaks thin, giving way to a wide expanse of
rolling grasses where a trail winds roughly alongside Nimrodel's banks. Some
large flat rocks have been placed in a row from north to south.
name: A Softly Flowing Stream

desc:
The air is heavy with the fresh scents of new growth emanating from the
unusually lush forest spreading from the south. Nestled here in proximity to the
forest are mounded clumps of tall-growing grass thick enough to completely
envelop a hobbit or even a dwarf.
name: Verdant Grasslands

desc:
In contrast to the tumbled highlands to the west, the land smoothes and slopes
in gentle grassy waves east towards Lothlorien. Pale flowers are sprinkled among
the verdure, and the air is as fresh and new as an everlasting spring day.
name: Gently Sloping Grasslands

desc:
Rolling in soft curves, the land spreads out from the feet of the Misty
Mountains, sloping gently towards the Golden Wood. Small tufts of grass grow
wherever they find a promising foothold, interspersed with clumps of hardy,
low-lying plants. Towering to the west are the omnipresent spires of the
Hithaeglir.
name: Rolling Lowlands

desc:
Rising slowly from the low-ground to the east, then sloping more steeply upward
to the ridge to the north, this area is, in the main, devoid of vegetation.
Nonetheless some hardy strands of grass and tough weeds poke from the grainy
soil.
name: A Gently Rising Slope

desc:
Lush grasses and alpine flowers carpet the loamy soil in clothes of the freshest
green, offering homes for small thick-furred creatures burrowed snugly into the
earth. A small foot-trail winds northwest, climbing slowly towards the spires of
the Misty Mountains.
name: Verdant Grasses

desc:
Shrubs begin to grow among the tall meadows that fade into the west, framed by
the peaks of the Misty Mountains. Mostly broad-leafed, sprawling bushes, they
gladly drink their fill from the Silverlode, crowding up against her flanks and
jostling for the best position to gather water and feed off the rich silt that
is deposited on her banks.
name: Shrubs alongside Silverlode

desc:
The trees here grow tall and majestic, elder scions, though not yet mellyrn.
Nearly as grand are the stands of oak and beech, even a few drooping willows
close unto Nimrodel, drawing their sustenance from her peerless waters. A trail
mirrors her progress, wide enough and free enough of weeds and debris that it
seems to still be used, if not often.
name: Beside the Flowing Stream

desc:
A soft tinkling emanates from the waterfall to the south, intimating in some
fashion the gentle renewal of dreams both forgotten and omnipresent. Mist and
vapour swirls upward from the falls below, dancing dreamily amidst the
tree-branches, depositing dewy droplets on the leaves. A good distance below,
aquamarine pools reflect whorls of colour, transforming into new patterns over
and over, infinitely creating and recreating themselves in the turbulent flow.
name: By the Falls of Nimrodel

desc:
The forest begins to grow more closely together, the trees gathering first in
small clumps, then becoming more plentiful to the south. A mixture of trees grow
in this area, tall, broad-leafed and deciduous in the main, but a few conifers
are intermingled here and there. Nimrodel runs beside the forest, wending her
way along a small declivity. A small footpath leads off to the north, joining
here with a larger trail.
name: Forest Borderlands

desc:
A trail shaded by flourishing trees and green bushes faithfully follows the
Nimrodel's whirling waters to the south and east. The river flows haphazard and
wayward as a butterfly's path. Nonetheless, its purpose is apparent as she winds
her way ever onward to the meeting with Silverlode, Anduin, and thence to the
sea.
name: Along a Flowing Stream

desc:
Huddled in secret conclave, the wind whispering between their branches with a
gentle murmur, these grand trees seem as if they might at any time pull their
roots from the ground and march away like a troop of huorns. Indeed the trees'
trunks are twisted into strange caricatures, like the craggy faces of dwarves or
trolls, with knots and protrusions for eyes, noses, and mouths. Through and
around the trees a small trail winds along the banks of Nimrodel; from east to
south and upwards to the Misty Mountains.
name: A Gathering of Trees

desc:
Vast and hoary, with huge limbs splayed far and high from its impressive trunk,
a grand old oak tree stands above all others. Its lesser brethren nestle close
under its eaves, almost as if seeking protection and council from their elder
kin. Close to the trees, Nimrodel winds merrily, her waters dappled lightly with
the illumination filtering through the trees.
name: Near a Grand Old Oak

desc:
Trees clutter the fields in increasingly dense patterns draped across the
rolling glens. Mixtures of oak and alder, rowan and pine, co-inhabit the
landscape with the tossing grasses and patches of scrub and heather. To the
southeast the forest darkens.
name: A Mixture of Trees

desc:
Bescattered with scree and detritus tumbled from the craggy heights directly to
your north, the ground here is by and large devoid of vegetation. Nevertheless,
a few strands of grass and other greenery emerge from the shards of rock that
perhaps conceal more hospitable soil beneath. Surely something fair must lie
beneath the windswept flats, for close to the south and east lush growths
flourish freely.
name: Rock-strewn Flats

desc:
Grand old oaks and pines grow thickly between the two streams, their branches
joining together; leaf and needle mingling together and casting mottled shadows
upon the ground. To the west the ground rises sharply to a cliff overlooking
Nimrodel. Weaving under the trees is a small trail, at times a mere animal path
under the low hanging branches.
name: Between the Rivers

desc:
A small belt of land stretches between two rivers, filled with brush, sand,
sharp rocks, and trees. The rivers have eroded this fork of land, and as a
consequence the terrain is difficult to travel. An old, overgrown trail runs
through the area, easily followed in some places and hidden beneath fallen trees
and brush in others.
name: A Fork of Land

desc:
Silverlode runs lazily here over a wide, shallow rock bed, shallow enough to
allow fording, given a little care in one's step. A loud rumbling to the south
signals the imminent merging of Silverlode with her sister-waters of Nimrodel.
name: A Wide, Rocky Streambed

desc:
The trees thin momentarily, giving way to an expanse of tufted river-grasses
waving sleepily in the breeze. The land is muted and quiet, except for the
excited muttering of water rushing. An old, but distinct trail winds through the
trees; it appears to receive some use, albeit infrequently.
name: An Expanse of Soft, Loamy Turf

desc:
Celebrant rushes by, spraying the rocky shore with moisture, nurturing clinging
masses of bright green mosses and lichens. It is difficult to hear anything over
the playful rush of the river as it traverses large tumbled and polished
boulders.
name: Upon Moss-padded Rocks

desc:
The trees grow thick here, and one can barely make one's way in between them. It
is reminiscent of a labyrinth of mighty tree-trunks, aged and unwithered. The
trees have been standing here for eon upon eon, though the tiniest signs of
decay hint that they have not been here forever.
name: Thickly Clustered Trees

desc:
The stream begins to fan out, widening significantly as it proceeds over a
glistening bed of polished milk quartz. Pebbles and larger rocks have been
polished by the flowing water to a lustrous sheen. The tall trees of Lothlorien
stand on the both sides of the arching river.
name: A Crystalline Streambed

desc:
A wide expanse of sand, nurturing only a few blades of fibrous grasses, spreads
here, evidently deposited by the Silverlode river. Strange tracks run here and
there in the loose sand, betraying the nearby river to be a frequent watering
place for nearby animals.
name: Sand Flats

desc:
Lush oaks, their branches drooping with the weight of their hearty growth,
cluster in thick stands on this small overlook. The ground is covered by roots,
bushes, and other vegetation that grow here in abundance. A faint tinkling noise
of flowing water can be heard from the north.
name: A Verdant Stand of Oaks

desc:
A few bird nests can be spotted in the trees. The dense canopy of branches
blocks out the sun almost completely, making it hard for the sun-loving plants
to survive on the forest floor. An opening in the canopy has formed here, as an
ancient tree succumbed to the circle of life, creating an opportunity for small
trees to compete for the small amount of sunlight that passes through the
opening.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Many different kinds of trees grow here. Pine, oak, maple and other species
struggle for life. Thick needles of pine-trees cover the ground, forming a dense
carpet. When the wind blows, leaves and branches sway back and forth, making all
kinds of sounds one can only hear in forests.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Heavily networked branches cast a thick shadow on the ground. Rarely can the eye
of a traveller spot a mallorn-tree around here, but to the north and the east
the mellyrn seem to increase. Sounds of wildlife come from the west, but in the
eastern direction the forest seems quieter.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Mellyrn can be seen everywhere, mixing with the other trees. Their great golden
crowns extend upwards in a breath-taking view. The thin branches of the graceful
trees support a shimmering sea of leaves. In some places tree-roots have come
out of the ground, as if in attempt to obstruct passage through the woods by
sending unwary travellers tumbling to the forest floor.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
The trees here grow hoary and grand, their limbs encrusted with mottled lichens
and draped with verdant drooping mosses. The turf is a soft springy carpet of
verdant green, nurtured by fair weather, rich soil, and the close proximity of
Nimrodel. Here and there are sprinklings of tufted pink flowers, their heads
nodding gently in the breeze.
name: Beside the Nimrodel

desc:
This high bluff is covered with venerable trees, their wide branches drooping
over the rushing waters below. The ground is richly covered with lush
vegetation. A curiously shaped granite rock offers a spot that looks inviting
and comfortable to sit on, should one be so inclined.
name: A Bluff above the Waters

desc:
The trees rise upward to a dizzying height, their branches interlocking to form
a thick canopy that blocks out all illumination to the forest floor. No smaller
trees grow here, nor are there many ground-plants, as the lack of illumination
has stunted their growth. Eventually one of the mighty trees shall fall, leaving
a window of sunshine to foster new growth.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
Although this forest grows thick and healthy, it bears only a passing
resemblance to the far older, grander forests of Beleriand. Though these ancient
giants seem timeless, even they will fade and be forgotten in time.
name: Wistful Grove

desc:
Many mellyrn grow here, mixing with oaks and elms. The golden trees grow
together in clumps, as if to protect whatever lies in their centre. The slender
trunks and branches of the mellyrn contrast sharply with the thicker trunks of
the other trees. Occasional bird calls echo down from the thick canopy of
tree-branches. A carpet of fallen leaves blankets the ground.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
The silvery boles of the mellyrn rise, tall and slender, into the shimmering
canopy of green and gold. The leaves of the trees rustle gently though no wind
reaches the ground where the air is still and fragrant with the scents of many
flowers and grasses.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
Great mallorn-trees extend upwards for hundreds of feet. Fallen golden leaves
cover the soft green grass. Small mallorn saplings stretch their slender
branches to the sky. While the forest appears ancient, no fallen trees can be
seen and the standing trees bear no traces of aging or decay. A soft mixed scent
is in the air, like the scent of new grass and flowers in springtime.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
A few grey rocks lie here, partly covered in thick green moss in which some
small insects can be seen jumping and crawling. Though this forest is apparently
ancient, the weathered rocks are its only part that shows any signs of aging.
The golden mellyrn that grow here are of a great height, yet their bark is
smooth and firm as that of saplings and there are no fallen trees or
tree-stumps. Many small bushes and shrubs grow by the silvery boles of the
mallorn-trees.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
The golden trees seem very vibrant here as a powerful river sweeps by, feeding
the forest with fresh water. The wind carries the smell of water as it travels
by. The soft sound of the river lapping against the shoreline can be heard from
the north and east.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
A few scattered mellyrn dip their roots gladly into the pristine waters of
Celebrant as she flows swift and true towards Anduin and ever onward to the sea.
The forest thickens rapidly as one recedes from the banks on either side.
name: By the Riverside

desc:
The tall trees of Lothlorien rise to great heights. Between and behind the
silvery trunks a flowing river makes a wide turn, running past to the north and
east. The riverside is spotted with mossy rocks and small bushes that grow in
dense clusters next to the arching river bend. The forest grows denser away from
the sparkling waters of the river.
name: Forested Riverside

desc:
A few low-lying conifers and scrub oaks huddle under the shade of some ancient
rowans, their high-tiered crowns far above you. This forest looks old, old
beyond reckoning, where the trees despite the cycle of life appear as old as the
rocks. Rough green beards still adorn the tall mountain oaks, with flowers and
berries as their mantles.
name: Within an Ancient Forest

desc:
Tall and stark, hoary oaks reach their remaining branches far above the floor of
the forest. Below them, humbler trees vie for the remaining light, while
underneath all is a blanket of leaves. All about you the forest spreads in every
direction, making one route seem as good as any other.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
A variety of oaks, ash, and lindens grow tall like elder statesmen, their rough
bark cracked and furrowed with age. Their tall crowns reach for the sky far
above, with faint beams of light illuminating a carpet of mosses and clumped
plants spread below.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
Tall and grand, with curtains of dry moss hanging like beards, the trees here
stand a bit taller and appear a bit older than elsewhere in the surrounding
forest. Oaks, ash, and a wayward rowan all rise proudly above the mottled
undergrowth, their long limbs rocking back and forth in time to some vernal
rhythm.
name: Elder Forest

desc:
Fresh and pristine like the first day of spring regardless the season, this
section of forest looks like it has not changed in a thousand years, yet there
is no palpable aura of agedness about the place. In a strange, Elven fashion it
seems both old and young at once.
name: Ageless Forest

desc:
Each trunk, bough, and leaf is an exercise in beauty; delicately precise yet
disdainful of contrived handicraft. Instead there is a blended quality of exact
unyielding precision coupled with organic boundlessness: a true expression of
creativity polished to magnificence.
name: A Pristine Stand of Trees

desc:
Soft light illuminates a golden carpet of leaves, belying the dense, deep shade
of woven canopies above. Like a story told and retold, yet always retaining its
initial energy, these ancient woods follow the pattern of the seasons.
name: Golden Mellyrn

desc:
A dark mere, ringed with a dense ring of trees, occupies a small bowl-shaped
depression amidst the forest. The pool's surface is laced with algae and small
lily-pads, and reflects beams of radiance back upwards to the trees'
outstretched limbs and the sky beyond.
name: A Tree-lined Pool

desc:
Mellyrn intermingle with bushes and other vegetation, forming dense walls
coloured in shades of brown and silver, green and gold. Tall and grand do the
trees rise, their crowns a symphony of whispering leaves dancing in the breeze.
A narrow pathway weaves between trunks and bushes to the west, and a round, dark
maw awaits to the north, leading into a cavern beneath the forest's floor.
name: Aged Mellyrn

desc:
A small cave opens up under the dark soil of Lothlorien. The cave is about five
yards wide and ten deep. A few twisting roots hang from the ceiling. The floor
has been stomped smooth, only some rocks litter the ground. The exit out is
located between two huge boulders to the south.
name: Cave under the Forest

desc:
The waters widen into a crystal-clear pool before spilling in a sparkling
curtain over dark, time-polished boulders of basalt. The splashing echoes off a
nearby cliff-face encrusted with lichen and petite lavender blooms. To either
side of the stream, the forest grows lush and green, nurtured and nourished by
the cool, pure waters of the Nimrodel.
name: At a Glistening Pool

desc:
Falling with a gentle sigh into a narrow declivity, Nimrodel rushes onward, her
progress constrained by the steep-sided gorge. The sound of her passage resounds
and builds with an echoing of burbles and murmurs that linger long after the
initial sound has faded.
name: A Wending Gorge

desc:
Fresh and bright as a new day dawning, sparkling in tones of blue, Nimrodel
careens north, twisting waywardly over and around rocks and obstructions. Great,
grand old trees stand watch along her banks, ever dutiful as the centuries flow
by.
name: Nimrodel

desc:
Soft, musical tones accompany Nimrodel's journey over polished rocks and gravel,
the music sad and wistful, yet compelling and beautiful nonetheless. Her fate,
though tragic, does nothing to diminish the memory of her loveliness.
name: Nimrodel

desc:
Silverlode winds on, pausing only to deposit a large field of sand on the
southern shore. A variety of fish dart through the waters. Grand old oaks and
pines shroud the southern bank with their crowns of green; roots dip gingerly
into the river's water.
name: Twisting Silverlode

desc:
Silverlode froths and shimmers as her path rolls over shining rockbeds, among
which silver-mailed fish frolic in the crystalline waters. Southwards, the land
rises up sharply, forming a rocky, tree-covered patch of land. To the north, a
glade of trees obscure the view.
name: Silverlode

desc:
Tumbling over the falls to the east, the clear water sings and glitters like
falling silver. The spray from the falls soaks both banks of the river making
the ground here soft underfoot. It is said that the voice of Nimrodel herself
can still heard in the singing of the water.
name: The Falls of Nimrodel

desc:
Re-forming again from the mists of the falls, Nimrodel moves with swiftness and
surety towards the east. The clear water splashes on the rocks of the river
bend, and ambient light scatters from the waves on her surface, forming
occasional tiny rainbows.
name: Below a Waterfall

desc:
Nimrodel flows now with haste and determination, drawing nigh to her sister-
waters, Silverlode. Her banks widen, and she gladly joins arms to form the
mighty Celebrant; her crystal waters swiftly intermingling and rushing ever
onward, towards the distant sea.
name: Nimrodel

desc:
Wide and strong flows Celebrant, newly joined by Nimrodel's influx, as she nears
a bend to the east, the ambient light scattered in random spray from her
rippling surface, creates tiny quivering rainbows under the light of the sun.
name: A Confluence of Waters

desc:
The river slows somewhat as it turns eastward, rolling ever onward towards the
Anduin. Unstoppable yet gentle in her progress, Celebrant graces the woods with
her soft murmurings and life-sustaining waters. White-grey granite boulders line
the shoreline on both sides.
name: The River Celebrant

desc:
Large and well-smoothed boulders rise about the water's surface, crowned around
with sprays and plumes of mist as Celebrant rushes past and beside them. The
sound is nigh overwhelming, so much so that it is near impossible to hear
anything else amid the roaring waters.
name: The River Celebrant

desc:
The waters of fair Celebrant rush insistently onward, over broken boulders worn
smooth by the river. As the life-giving water breaks on the rocks, it sends
plumes of water onto the fertile soil that line the shores. A white granite
boulder sits in mid-stream, causing the cascading waters to divide and then
rejoin, all in but a moment of time.
name: The River Celebrant

desc:
Flowing wide and strong, the Celebrant slices through heavily forested banks.
Tall, silver-barked trees sway gently in the breeze, almost as if dancing to
some enchanting melody. The water sparkles and glitters as it flows over a bed
of smooth, rounded stones.
name: The River Celebrant

desc:
Here the valley floor reaches its lowest elevation, and judging by the terrain,
the spring thaws must bring at least a few small rivulets of water through the
area. Thus it is that a small stand of trees manages to cling to life here. A
lone oak of extreme age holds its proud head high amid a cluster of far younger
pines and firs. Perhaps this ancient tree once stood at the borders of Lorien,
but now the Golden Wood and the power within it have both faded and diminished.
name: A Solitary Stand of Trees

desc:
The valley floor lies here at some distance beneath the foot of the cliffs. What
might once have been a narrow, stony trail tracks along the edge of the sheer
rock face. The ground is littered by rubble which has perhaps fallen from the
wall, and indeed the latter is pock-marked by many pits.
name: Along a Cliff Face

desc:
Winding repeatedly, Nimrodel swirls to the east, her waters a mesmerizing blur
of blue and white; a wild, churning melody surrounds her advance. A promenade of
trees line her progress, their long branches drooping in reverence, bowed yet
alert, reminiscent of ears heeding a song only faintly heard, yet all the more
insistent for its subtly. Close to the east, a soft roar fills the air.
name: Nimrodel

desc:
An earthy cave, supported by a vault of strongly intertwined roots of silvery
grey seems to show signs of recent habitation. Tufts of golden fur line the
packed dirt floor, and some gnawed bones and fruit pits are scattered in a
particular corner. Opposite this corner is a bed of grass, apparently gathered
by a host who desires his comforts.
name: An Earthy Cave
